title: Tuning of quantum nanoscaled friction within the Prandtl-Tomlinson model
abstract: Nanoscaled friction is a fundamental tribological phenomenon with complex behavior of its dynamical force. Here, we utilize the Prandtl-Tomlinson framework to investigate systematically the different means of control of the frictional force at the quantum and classical levels. It is found that the frictional dynamics can be controlled by the corrugation and characteristic length ratio parameters dependent upon properties of the nanoparticle-chain system. In addition to the stick-slip regime, other types of motion are uncovered, highlighting the richness of the frictional dynamics. The importance of Landau-Zener tunneling for the quantum motion is also analyzed. These findings provide valuable insights for interpreting experimental observations and controlling quantum frictional behavior in nanoscale systems.
summary: This paper explores how to control nanoscale friction by adjusting system parameters like corrugation and length ratios. It identifies new motion regimes and highlights how quantum effects like Landau-Zener tunneling influence frictional dynamics. These insights are crucial for controlling friction in future nanotechnological applications.
main_problem: Investigating the mechanisms for controlling frictional force at both quantum and classical levels in nanoscale systems.
main_result: The study identifies that frictional dynamics can be tuned via corrugation and characteristic length ratio parameters, and uncovers motion types beyond the standard stick-slip regime, including the role of Landau-Zener tunneling.
method: Theoretical investigation using the Prandtl-Tomlinson framework to analyze dynamical force behavior.
model_or_system: A nanoparticle-chain system modeled within the Prandtl-Tomlinson framework to study nanoscale friction.
key_observables: Frictional force, dynamical force behavior, and types of motion (e.g., stick-slip).
important_parameters: Corrugation, characteristic length ratio, and Landau-Zener tunneling probability.
paper_structure: The paper utilizes the Prandtl-Tomlinson framework to systematically investigate control means of friction, explores different motion regimes, and analyzes the impact of quantum tunneling.
